entries.statement: AXIOM (the grade set): the confidence order has the named positions ungraded, asserted, supported, corroborated, and the settled tier (checked, independently-rechecked, constitutive), which collapse to the five-level working line ungraded, asserted, supported, corroborated, settled. This set is taken as given.

canonical.statement: AXIOM (the grade set): the confidence order has the named positions ungraded, asserted, supported, corroborated, and the settled tier (checked, independently-rechecked, constitutive), which collapse to the five-level working line ungraded, asserted, supported, corroborated, settled. This set is taken as given.

entries.statement: AXIOM (the partial order): on the collapsed working line the order is total, ungraded < asserted < supported < corroborated < settled, and the settled tier is a branch above corroborated rather than a single point. This order is taken as given.

canonical.statement: AXIOM (the partial order): on the collapsed working line the order is total, ungraded < asserted < supported < corroborated < settled, and the settled tier is a branch above corroborated rather than a single point. This order is taken as given.

entries.statement: AXIOM (mode incomparability): within the settled tier the empirical mode (checked < independently-rechecked) and the constitutive mode are in different modes and are incomparable, so comparing two settled positions of different mode is undefined. This incomparability is taken as given.

canonical.statement: AXIOM (mode incomparability): within the settled tier the empirical mode (checked < independently-rechecked) and the constitutive mode are in different modes and are incomparable, so comparing two settled positions of different mode is undefined. This incomparability is taken as given.

entries.statement: THEOREM (meet commutative): meet(a, b) = meet(b, a) for all grades a, b on the collapsed working line, where meet is the weakest-of, the lower of the two by collapsed rank (kernel/schema/confidence.mjs).

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.statement: THEOREM (meet commutative): meet(a, b) = meet(b, a) for all grades a, b on the collapsed working line, where meet is the weakest-of, the lower of the two by collapsed rank (kernel/schema/confidence.mjs).

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

entries.statement: THEOREM (meet associative): meet(meet(a, b), c) = meet(a, meet(b, c)) for all grades a, b, c on the collapsed working line.

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.statement: THEOREM (meet associative): meet(meet(a, b), c) = meet(a, meet(b, c)) for all grades a, b, c on the collapsed working line.

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

entries.statement: THEOREM (meet idempotent): meet(a, a) = a for every grade a on the collapsed working line.

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.statement: THEOREM (meet idempotent): meet(a, a) = a for every grade a on the collapsed working line.

entries.statement: THEOREM (join commutative): join(a, b) = join(b, a) for all grades a, b, where join is the strongest-of, the higher of the two by collapsed rank (kernel/schema/confidence.mjs).

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

entries.statement: THEOREM (join associative): join(join(a, b), c) = join(a, join(b, c)) for all grades a, b, c.

canonical.statement: THEOREM (join associative): join(join(a, b), c) = join(a, join(b, c)) for all grades a, b, c.

entries.statement: THEOREM (join idempotent): join(a, a) = a for every grade a.

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.statement: THEOREM (join idempotent): join(a, a) = a for every grade a.

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

entries.statement: THEOREM (absorption): meet(a, join(a, b)) = a and join(a, meet(a, b)) = a for all grades a, b, the absorption laws relating meet and join.

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.statement: THEOREM (absorption): meet(a, join(a, b)) = a and join(a, meet(a, b)) = a for all grades a, b, the absorption laws relating meet and join.

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

entries.statement: THEOREM (lattice): the five-level collapsed grade set under meet and join is a lattice, the conjunction the commutativity, associativity, idempotence, and absorption laws establish by exhaustion over all pairs and triples.

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.statement: THEOREM (lattice): the five-level collapsed grade set under meet and join is a lattice, the conjunction the commutativity, associativity, idempotence, and absorption laws establish by exhaustion over all pairs and triples.

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

entries.statement: THEOREM (meet and join well-defined under mode incomparability): meet and join collapse the settled tier to a single rank before comparing, so they are total on the collapsed line even though checked, independently-rechecked, and constitutive are incomparable across modes in the full order; the operations never produce a false ordering between two incomparable settled positions.

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

canonical.statement: THEOREM (meet and join well-defined under mode incomparability): meet and join collapse the settled tier to a single rank before comparing, so they are total on the collapsed line even though checked, independently-rechecked, and constitutive are incomparable across modes in the full order; the operations never produce a false ordering between two incomparable settled positions.

checking_records.method: exhaustive enumeration of meet and join over the whole collapsed grade line

entries.statement: PROPERTY (earned-grade recurrence): the earned grade of a claim is, per support group, the weakest-of (meet) of each member's min(its own earned grade, its link's declared grade); then the strongest-of (join) across groups; lifted to corroborated when two groups with disjoint source footprints each deliver at least supported (the independence lift); combined with the claim's own basis (a distinct-party checking record or a constitutive kind) by the two-row rule; and capped by the kind's ceiling, as computed by kernel/grounding/earned-grade.mjs.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.statement: PROPERTY (earned-grade recurrence): the earned grade of a claim is, per support group, the weakest-of (meet) of each member's min(its own earned grade, its link's declared grade); then the strongest-of (join) across groups; lifted to corroborated when two groups with disjoint source footprints each deliver at least supported (the independence lift); combined with the claim's own basis (a distinct-party checking record or a constitutive kind) by the two-row rule; and capped by the kind's ceiling, as computed by kernel/grounding/earned-grade.mjs.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

entries.statement: PROPERTY (linear resolution): the earned grade is memoized per claim identity, so each claim resolves once and each support edge is followed once; a full resolution is linear in the support graph, order V plus E.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.statement: PROPERTY (linear resolution): the earned grade is memoized per claim identity, so each claim resolves once and each support edge is followed once; a full resolution is linear in the support graph, order V plus E.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

entries.statement: PROPERTY (ungrouped supports are singletons): a support with no support_group is its own singleton group, an independent alternative, never collected with other ungrouped supports, so ungrouped supports combine strongest-of rather than weakest-of.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.statement: PROPERTY (ungrouped supports are singletons): a support with no support_group is its own singleton group, an independent alternative, never collected with other ungrouped supports, so ungrouped supports combine strongest-of rather than weakest-of.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

entries.statement: PROPERTY (cycle guard): a support cycle resolves the in-cycle claim to asserted, because the memoization sets the cache to asserted before recursing, so the earned-grade computation terminates deterministically on cyclic support.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.statement: PROPERTY (cycle guard): a support cycle resolves the in-cycle claim to asserted, because the memoization sets the cache to asserted before recursing, so the earned-grade computation terminates deterministically on cyclic support.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

entries.statement: PROPERTY (settledness not inherited): a claim resting on settled support delivers at most corroborated; only the claim's own basis, a distinct-party check or a constitutive kind, reaches the settled tier, and the kind ceiling enforces this.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.statement: PROPERTY (settledness not inherited): a claim resting on settled support delivers at most corroborated; only the claim's own basis, a distinct-party check or a constitutive kind, reaches the settled tier, and the kind ceiling enforces this.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

entries.statement: PROPERTY (determinism): the earned grade is a deterministic function of the graph, independent of the order supports or groups are visited, because meet and join are commutative and associative and the memoization is order-independent.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.statement: PROPERTY (determinism): the earned grade is a deterministic function of the graph, independent of the order supports or groups are visited, because meet and join are commutative and associative and the memoization is order-independent.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

entries.statement: PROPERTY (contamination monotone, single pass): contamination propagates monotonically along support edges by the weakest-of rule, so a contested support can only lower a claim's grade, never raise it, and the memoized resolution reaches the fixpoint in one pass on the acyclic support graph.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.statement: PROPERTY (contamination monotone, single pass): contamination propagates monotonically along support edges by the weakest-of rule, so a contested support can only lower a claim's grade, never raise it, and the memoized resolution reaches the fixpoint in one pass on the acyclic support graph.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

entries.statement: PROPERTY (crossing min): a cross-kernel crossing grades a composite claim as min(ceiling, min over its necessary carried grades) under the meet (compositeGrade in kernel/composition/transfer.mjs), folding from the top of scale, so the cross-boundary grade is well-defined on the collapsed line even across settled grades incomparable within their modes; corroborating citations are visible but not folded.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.statement: PROPERTY (crossing min): a cross-kernel crossing grades a composite claim as min(ceiling, min over its necessary carried grades) under the meet (compositeGrade in kernel/composition/transfer.mjs), folding from the top of scale, so the cross-boundary grade is well-defined on the collapsed line even across settled grades incomparable within their modes; corroborating citations are visible but not folded.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

entries.statement: PROPERTY (untyped floor, fork restores): a crossing into a kernel that does not pin the same type-hash arrives untyped and grounds nothing until an owned fork adopts the type locally, which restores standing; a same-hash crossing composes native and lossless.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

canonical.statement: PROPERTY (untyped floor, fork restores): a crossing into a kernel that does not pin the same type-hash arrives untyped and grounds nothing until an owned fork adopts the type locally, which restores standing; a same-hash crossing composes native and lossless.

checking_records.method: differential testing: the code versus the extracted recurrence over random support graphs

entries.statement: PROPERTY (certificate seals the bundle): the gate receipt's certificate hash is a function of the sealed certificate bundle (the accepted claim identities and their grades, the binding table, the checking records in play, the store state verified against, and the ruleset version, schema version, and contribution hash), changing if and only if a bundled part changes and reproducing byte-for-byte for an identical certified assembly; the excluded volatile fields (findings, the decision label, the closures and matches) do not affect it. Computed downstream of grounding by certificateSeal in kernel/gate/gate.mjs, so it adds no cost to the earned-grade recurrence and its resolution stays linear.

checking_records.method: differential testing of the seal: perturb each bundled part and confirm the certificate hash moves, and confirm an identical certified assembly reproduces the hash

canonical.method: differential testing of the seal: perturb each bundled part and confirm the certificate hash moves, and confirm an identical certified assembly reproduces the hash

Computed downstream of grounding by certificateSeal in kernel/gate/gate.mjs, so it adds no cost to the earned-grade recurrence and its resolution stays linear.

sources.description: The EpiStack confidence order and protocol specification (kernel/schema/confidence.mjs, docs/protocol-spec.md Section 3): the grade set, its partial order, and the empirical/constitutive mode split, declared as this kernel's given primitives

PLACEHOLDER CLASS (source-class decision, case b): a differential-test harness that generates random graphs and confirms the extracted math is what the code computes. The substrate menu has no differential-testing class; primary-measurement is the closest honest existing class pending a maintainer decision

PLACEHOLDER CLASS (source-class decision, case b): exhaustive enumeration of a finite operation over its whole domain, a proof by exhaustion. The substrate menu has no exhaustive-check class; primary-measurement is the closest honest existing class pending a maintainer decision on whether the substrate menu gains one

PLACEHOLDER CLASS (source-class decision, case b): a formal proof-assistant proof. The substrate menu has no formal-proof class; primary-measurement is the closest honest existing class pending a maintainer decision, and it is the least natural fit of the three placeholders
